title: pH and Buffer Chemistry
content: pH = −log₁₀[H⁺]. Buffer solutions resist pH change via conjugate acid-base pairs (Henderson-Hasselbalch: pH = pKa + log([A⁻]/[HA])). pH electrodes exhibit a Nernstian response: E = E₀ + (RT/F)·ln[H⁺], giving ~59.16 mV/pH at 25 °C. Temperature dependence of pH is approximately −0.003 pH units/°C for neutral water, requiring temperature compensation for accurate control.
